purpose: Canonical source registry for the three healing modes: Ayurveda, Traditional Chinese Medicine, and Homeopathy, plus supporting general herbal references.

app_scope.ranking_target: source-supported traditional-system relevance, not medical truth
app_scope.safety_position: educational, citation-based practitioner research support; not diagnosis or prescription

core_policy: Core guiding medicine texts are limited to the user-approved canon in docs/CORE_BOOK_CANON.md. Prior generic or ChatGPT-suggested candidates are retained only as excluded audit entries unless the user explicitly re-approves them.

local_file_status.present: A raw/local source file exists in the repo workspace.
local_file_status.missing: No raw/local source file is present yet.
local_file_status.private_present: A local private source exists but must not be committed or exposed as extracted text.

ingestion_status.raw_only: Raw source exists but has not been ingested to final outputs.
ingestion_status.missing_source: Source must be located or supplied before ingestion.
ingestion_status.metadata_only: Only metadata should be tracked unless rights are clarified.

priority.P0: Required for minimum viable Pattern App mode coverage.
priority.P1: High-priority expansion for clinical or materia medica coverage.
priority.P2: Useful later expansion or comparison layer.

sources.title: Boericke's New Manual of Homeopathic Materia Medica with Repertory
sources.rights_access_status: Homeoint structured web source accessed as public web pages; verify terms before publishing extracted full text. Archive asset also needs access verification.
sources.text_quality_notes: User-designated core practical Homeopathy reference. Homeoint entries parsed as one JSONL chunk per remedy with section headings preserved where available.
sources.next_action: Use Boericke as the first source-backed Homeopathy remedy-differential layer; add Kent-style repertory/rubric data next for stronger ranking.

sources.title: Kent's Final General Repertory
sources.rights_access_status: Homeoint structured web source accessed as public web pages; verify terms before publishing extracted full text. Modern edited Kent repertory editions may be copyrighted and must remain separate.
sources.text_quality_notes: Selected Kent repertory sections parsed from Homeoint into rubric JSONL with rubric path, page, remedy abbreviations, and source URL.
sources.next_action: Use selected Kent rubrics to support Homeopathy rubric clusters; expand parsing/normalization and map abbreviations to full remedy names.

next_development_order: Validate this registry against local files.
Create schema validation tests for source registry and chunk outputs.
Normalize existing Charaka, Ashtanga Hridayam, Huang Di Nei Jing, and Organon chunks to the master metadata schema.

Build first retrieval/ranking prototype only after citations and schema validation are stable.
